channel.title: Windows on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Windows on yexca'Blog

item.title: Remove Windows Network Addresses (FTP) from Quick Access
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

No direct fix, but you can clear all custom Quick Access entries (resets to default).

Go to C:\\Users\\YourUsername\\AppData\\Roaming\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Recent\\AutomaticDestinations. Back up all files in that folder, then nuke ’em.

References

FTP address cannot be removed from Quick Access, other folders can – Microsoft Community

item.title: Windows Screenshots and Screen Recording
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview

Intro

These methods are for general use. For specialized requirements, you’ll need to do your own research.

1. Using Built-in Windows 10/11 Tools

1) The Built-in Snipping Tool

Windows 10/11 comes with a built-in app called “Snipping Tool.” You can use it in two ways.

<1> Launching the App

Press the Win key, open your app list, and find “Snipping Tool.”

item.title: Installing Official Android Subsystem on Win11
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash

This article was written by Hiyoung

Prerequisites: Windows 11 Pro or higher, set system region to United States.

In Settings -> Apps -> Optional features -> More Windows features, check ‘Virtual Machine Platform’, then restart your system.

Next, download the installation package. Link: https://pan.baidu.com/s/1215GlKeDCHcbE0I2SgtWLg Extraction code: frkx

After downloading, you’ll see two files: one is the Android Subsystem installer, the other is the WSA Toolbox used for installing APK files.
